problem_statement: A county mosquito-control route planner began producing implausibly long field routes just as a northern service zone was added. Dispatch needs to distinguish the expected increase from a planning defect before crews are sent to treatment sites.

observations.statement: Published route distance rose 78% on June 18, although the approved zone expansion added 6.2% more treatment sites and the planning estimate was a 5% to 8% distance increase.
observations.provenance: Dispatch planning dashboard and signed expansion estimate

observations.statement: Field supervisors verified 30 suspect site coordinates against survey sheets; the stored latitude, longitude, and coordinate reference identifier were correct before optimization.
observations.provenance: Survey-control comparison signed by field supervisors

observations.statement: The release inventory shows the application binary and route settings unchanged; the overnight package refresh changed GeoTransform from 4.8.1 to 4.8.2.
A standalone transformation of the vendor's reference coordinate swaps axes under 4.8.2, while 4.8.1 and the vendor's corrective 4.8.3 package return the surveyed point within two meters.
observations.provenance: Isolated package-compatibility test witnessed by GIS staff

observations.statement: Planner traces show correct surveyed coordinates entering the transformation boundary and axis-reversed projected coordinates leaving it for every implausible waypoint sampled.
observations.provenance: Planner boundary trace captured from copied route inputs

observations.statement: Vehicle constraints, depot locations, and road-closure feeds were unchanged, and a manual distance matrix for twelve sites differed from the incident planner by factors of four to nine.
observations.provenance: Dispatch configuration audit and GIS analyst distance worksheet

observations.statement: The package vendor's bundled corrective note identifies an axis-order regression in 4.8.2 for the coordinate reference used by the county.
observations.provenance: Corrective package release note retained in the software archive

observations.statement: The safety boundary suspended automatic route publication, retained operator-approved paper routes, and allowed package replacement only after coordinate and distance checks in an isolated planner.
A canary planner using 4.8.3 produced all 30 surveyed coordinates within two meters and route distances within 1.4% of the independent GIS calculation.

After the corrective package was promoted, all 42 routes passed supervisor review for five consecutive dispatches; total distance settled 6.4% above the old-zone baseline, consistent with the real expansion.
